body:
# notion-md automated demo This page is the durable live showcase for `@overeng/notion-md`: a local `.nmd` file synced 1:1 with a Notion page through the same CLI used by the E2E tests. ## Body surface - Stock Notion enhanced Markdown stays in the body. - Strict sync metadata stays in frontmatter. - Content-addressed merge evidence stays in `.notion-md/objects`. ## Editable Markdown ### Lists 1. Pull the Notion page into `demo/showcase.nmd`. 2. Edit the Markdown body locally. 3. Run guarded `push` or `sync`. - Bulleted content round-trips through the Markdown endpoint. - Local-only metadata is stripped before writing to Notion. ### Tasks - [x] Pull demo page - [x] Push local Markdown edits - [x] Verify clean status - [ ] Extend once file bytes and comment projection are implemented ### Callout > The demo intentionally avoids local frontmatter prose in the Notion body. If you can see this page in Notion, you are seeing only the synced Markdown body. ### Code ```typescript const command = 'notion-md sync packages/@overeng/notion-md/demo/showcase.nmd' ``` ### Table
Surface Local representation Current policy
Body Stock Notion enhanced Markdown Guarded two-way sync
Properties Strict frontmatter Modeled writable forms
Unknown blocks Frontmatter or object store Preserve or explicit delete
Review Roughdraft markup Refuse unresolved by default
## Conflict model `notion-md` compares base, local, and remote body hashes. Non-overlapping edits can merge automatically; overlapping edits produce a Roughdraft conflict artifact. ## Demo identity - Notion page: @overeng/notion-md demo (automated) - Local file: `packages/@overeng/notion-md/demo/showcase.nmd` ## Exhaustive feature showcase This bottom section is intentionally broad. It keeps the basic demo readable above, then exercises the body and frontmatter-backed surfaces we expect the sync tool to preserve. ### Inline formatting Plain text can mix **bold**, *italic*, ~~strikethrough~~, `inline code`, [links](https://developers.notion.com/), colored text, and highlighted text. Inline date mentions round-trip as enhanced Markdown: and . ### Headings and structure # Heading level 1 sample ## Heading level 2 sample ### Heading level 3 sample #### Heading level 4 sample --- The divider above is part of the Markdown body. It is not the `.nmd` frontmatter boundary because it appears after the local frontmatter block. ### Lists - Top-level bullet - Nested bullet - Deep nested bullet 1. Ordered item 1. Nested ordered item 2. Another nested ordered item - [x] Completed task - [ ] Open task ### Blocks > Quote block with a short note about guarded sync. Callout block with **rich text** and a child list. - Callout child bullet
Toggle block with **formatted** summary Toggle child paragraph. - Toggle child bullet
# Toggle heading sample {toggle="true" color="orange_bg"} Toggle heading child paragraph. ### Columns Column one paragraph. - Column one bullet Column two paragraph. 1. Column two ordered item ### Tables
Feature Representation Policy
Body Stock enhanced Markdown Guarded update
Frontmatter Strict JSON schema Stripped before body push
Objects Content-addressed refs Exact-byte validation
### Code and equations ```typescript const page = await notionMd.sync({ path: 'packages/@overeng/notion-md/demo/showcase.nmd', }) ``` Block equation: $$ E = mc^2 $$ Inline equation input is preserved conservatively until raw Notion rich-text evidence proves full Markdown endpoint fidelity: \$a\^2 + b\^2 = c\^2\$. ### Enhanced references - Page mention: @overeng/notion-md demo (automated) - Table of contents: ### Sync surface coverage
Notion feature Local surface Demo status
Paragraphs, headings, lists, quotes, dividers Body Markdown Shown above
Tasks, code, links, rich text, colors Body enhanced Markdown Shown above
Callouts, toggles, toggle headings, columns, tables, table of contents Body enhanced Markdown tags Shown above
Date mentions and page mentions Body enhanced Markdown tags Shown above
Page title, icon, cover, trash, lock Strict frontmatter page metadata Modeled outside body
Page properties: title, text, number, select, multi-select, status, date, people, files, checkbox, URL, email, phone, relation, place, verification Strict tagged frontmatter properties Modeled outside body
Unsupported blocks, bookmarks, embeds, link previews, child pages, child databases Unknown placeholders plus object storage Preserve or explicit delete
Comments and suggestions Future Roughdraft/Notion comment bridge Guarded; not silently pushed
Files and media bytes Future content-addressed file payloads Metadata modeled; durable bytes pending
### Local-only safety checks - Frontmatter is visible in the local file but never sent as body content. - Body pushes use Notion Markdown updates with stale-write checks. - Property and metadata pushes can proceed independently from remote body edits. - Unknown-block deletion requires explicit destructive intent. - Roughdraft review markers are refused by default so local review state does not leak into Notion.
